text: This work addresses the inherent instability of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) in alkaline electrolytes, a major bottleneck in its electrochemical conversion to 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid (FDCA). We demonstrate that HMF rapidly undergoes a base-catalyzed Cannizzaro reaction to form 5-hydroxymethyl-2-furancarboxylic acid (HMFCA) and 2,5-bis(hydroxymethyl)furan (DHMF). Unlike HMF, these intermediates are highly stable in strongly alkaline media and remain fully capable of being electrooxidized to FDCA. By leveraging this "indirect" oxidation pathway, we achieved efficient conversion at unprecedentedly high substrate and base concentrations. This strategy effectively suppresses humin formation, enhances atom economy, and enables current densities of approximately 1 A cm⁻², reaching levels suitable for industrial-scale processes.
